Find other locations at:
-Kentucky Drug Disposal Location Look Up https://odcp.ky.gov/Pages/Prescription-Drug-DisposalLocations.aspx
-DEA Drug Disposal Location Look Up https://apps.deadiversion.usdoj.gov/pubdispsearch/spring/
main?execution=e1s1
-Walgreen Pharmacies —Some Walgreens havea safe disposal program. Look up the closest
location at www.walgreens.com/storelocator/find.jsp?RxDisposal=true
If you are unable to access these locations, there are ways to safely throw away medications at home. Some medicines
can be flushed to prevent unintended access by others.
-Refer to the FDA website to check your specific medicine www.fda.gov
-The Kentucky Poison Control Center (www.kypoisoncontrol.com) can answer questions. Medical staff
are available are available through the hotline 24 hours a day at 1-[PHONE-3801].
Medication Side Effects:
* Breathing too slow or stopped
+ Drowsiness/tiredness
* Dizziness
* Constipation
* Nausea and vomiting
+ Itching
* Dry mouth
It is important to talk with your healthcare provider about any side effects you experience.
CALL 911 or have a family member call if:
* Breathing is slower than normal
* You cannot be awakened
¢ You have a seizure
Talk with your healthcare provider about other ways to manage pain:
Apply heat on the area of pain 20-30 minutes every 2 hours as directed to help decrease pain and muscle
spasms.
Consider other Medications. Talk to your healthcare provider about different medication options.
Go to physical therapy as directed. Physical therapist help teach you exercises to decrease pain and
improve your quality of life.
Exercise for 30 minutes, 3 times a week. Regular physical activity can decrease pain and improve
movement and strength.

Real people. Remarkable care.
* Get enough sleep. Create a bedtime routine. Go to sleep and wake up at the same time every day. Avoid
afternoon caffeine.
* Talk with a counselor or therapist. Untreated depression or anxiety can worsen pain symptoms. A type of
counseling called cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) can help your pain by changing the way you think about
it. CBT can also improve your mood, sleep, and the way you move.
Follow Up With Your Healthcare Provider:
* You will need to go to your provider often when taking these medicines. The provider will need to check to
see how the medicine is working. Make sure you go to all appointments.
* Call your provider if:
° Your medical problem has changed
° You are having side effects that are concerning to you. Some of the side effects can be helped by
changing the dose of the medicine.
° You have any questions or concerns

KORT Rehabilitation Services
You have been referred to KORT Rehabilitation services. KORT is a joint-venture entity with Norton Healthcare and Select
Medical. If you would like a referral to another entity please contact the practice for assistance.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.

BHMG Orthopaedic Surgery Clinic Note
Subjective
Pain of the Left Shoulder (Left shoulder pain; Ongoing since fall that occurred 4/26/19; Describes a burning sensation;
Pain 6/10-worsens with movement; Anti-inflammatories, ice/heat, wearing a sling provide some improvement.)
HPI
Amy Mills is a 35 y.o. female. Patient is here today for second opinion regarding her left shoulder and neck. On
4/26/2019, she slipped and fell in a bank injuring her shoulder and neck. Since that time, she has had difficulty getting
treatment she tells me. She has a burning sensation in the deltoid area and that radiates to her neck and down her
arm to her forearm and sometimes her fingers. She is seeing a neurosurgeon, Dr. Shields, in Louisville. She rates
her pain a 6 out of 10 with movement. She has stiffness in her neck. She has tried two therapy visits and it seems
that her therapist stop treatment more than her. She is tried ice and heat and anti-inflammatories. She is here at the
request of Teresa Gross.
Past Medical History:

* Abnormal Pap smear of cervix
¢ Urinary incontinence
* Uterus prolapse

Clinical Notes (continued)
Imaging/Studies
Imaging Results (Last 24 Hours)
** No results found for the last 24 hours. **
IMPRESSION:
1. Degenerative disc disease and small superimposed disc protrusion at
C3/4 causing moderate central canal and mild right-sided neural
foraminal stenosis.
2. Otherwise unremarkable exam.
This report was finalized on 6/22/2019 11:50 AM by Dr. Kevin J Croce,
MD.
IMPRESSION:
1. No definitive source for the patient's symptoms. No fracture
2. No evidence of rotator cuff tear.
This report was finalized on 8/23/2019 2:58 PM by Dr. Harold Reedy, MD.
We have also reviewed in addition to the MRI of her cervical spine and shoulder, plain x-rays of the patient's left
shoulder. They show AC joint arthritis with significant jointspace narrowing. Type II acromion.
Assessment:
Acute pain ofleft shoulder
Cervical radiculopathy
Central stenosis of spinal canal
Paresthesias
Adhesive capsulitis of left shoulder
Arthritis of left acromioclavicular joint
SY OP Sek
Plan:
1. Continue over-the-counter medication as needed for discomfort
Adhesive capsulitis left shoulder with AC joint arthritis--undoubtedly, the stiffness in the shoulder is contributing to
her AC joint pain. | recommended that she get her cervical spine squared away and this should alleviate a lot of
her symptoms that are coming from these 2 sites. We will hold off on any injections into the glenohumeral joint
and physical therapy as this is likely to exacerbate her underlying cervical spine condition. She has told me as
much today. | am happy to see her back in about 6 to 8 weeks to see how she is doing overall. | agree with the
plan for an EMG that her neurosurgeon has ordered. | will defer to the management of the C-spine condition to
him.
Janak Ramesh Talwalkar, MD

ee
Plan:
1. Continue over-the-counter medication as needed for discomfort
2. Adhesive capsulitis left shoulder with AC joint arthritis--undoubtedly, the stiffness in the shoulder is contributing
to her AC joint pain. | recommended that she get her cervical spine squared away and this should alleviate a lot
of her symptoms that are coming from these 2 sites. We will hold off on any injections into the glenohumeral
joint and physical therapy as this is likely to exacerbate her underlying cervical spine condition. She has told me
as much today. | am happy to see her back in about 6 to 8 weeks to see how she is doing overall. | agree with
the plan for an EMG that her neurosurgeon has ordered. | will defer to the management of the C-spine condition
to him.

Triage
Triage Information
Decision: None Schedule by date: 11/9/2019
Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following:
Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side ofthe body
Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding
Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
Sudden severe headache with no known cause
Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important toseek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a
stroke.
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away!
e Call 911
* Call a suicide hotline to speak to a counselor. 1-800-273-TALK or 1-800-SUICIDE
*** END OF REPORT ***

Clinical Notes (continued)
Amy Mills
[DOB-5810]
EP02256299
Date: [DOB-3572]
PERTINENT PAST HISTORY: This patient was last seen in the office on 10/08/19. This 35 yr/o female has a history of neck pain. She states that these symptoms began after a fall on 4/26/19 in which 
she slipped on a wet floor at a bank. She fell forward against a bench and hit the back of her head and her left 
shoulder. Immediately afterwards, she began to experience pain on the left side of her neck. In her last appointment, 
she also noted left arm pain extending from the upper arm to the mid forearm, and numbness in the fingers of the left 
hand, which also began after the fall. She feels that this pain is centered at her elbow. Today the patient complains of 
continued neck pain, as well as pain in the left upper arm. Patient notes weakness in the left arm and hand, especially 
on writing and typing. She is left handed. Strength in the right arm is normal. She has undergone 3 sessions of 
physical therapy to the left shoulder, from which she notes no relief of her pain. She notes some difficulties with 
balance. She experienced some bladder incontinence immediately following the injury, but states that bladder function 
has returned to normal. She has seen a neurologist regarding persistent headaches since her fall.
Previous conservative treatment includes medications (including Elavil, Norco, Voltaren). She has taken Gabapentin in 
the past but discontinued it due to side effects of nausea and vomiting.
ROS
Review of Systems 
Genitourinary: Positive for frequency. Musculoskeletal: Positive for arthralgias, back pain, gait problem, myalgias, neck pain and neck stiffness. Neurological: Positive for dizziness, weakness (both sides), light-headedness, numbness (left arm, leg and hip) and 
headaches. All other systems reviewed and are negative.

[DOB-3561] - Office Visit in Norton Neuroscience Institute (continued)
Clinical Notes (continued)
Reflexes: The deep tendon reflexes are 2/4 for the upper extremities. 
Sensory: Intact to pinprick sensation in the upper extremities.
Cerebellar: There was no dysmetria. Fine motor coordination was intact.
Gait: Gait was normal based without ataxia. Tandem, heel, and toe gait were intact. 
Skin: texture, temperature and coloration are WNL. 
IMAGING STUDIES: An EMG performed on 12/13/19 shows evidence of mild chronic radiculopathy of C5 on the left. A cervical MRI scan performed recently shows evidence of small disc bulge at C3-4, slightly distorting the spinal cord 
at that level. I personally and independently reviewed these films along with the radiologist report and discussed them 
in detail with the patient.
DIAGNOSIS: Cervical disc herniation at C3-4 
MEDICAL DECISION MAKING: This 35 yr/o female has a history of neck pain. She states that these symptoms 
began after a fall on 4/26/19 in which she slipped on a wet floor at a bank. She fell forward against a bench and hit the 
back of her head and her left shoulder. Immediately afterwards, she began to experience pain on the left side of her 
neck. In her last appointment, she also noted left arm pain extending from the upper arm to the mid forearm, and 
numbness in the fingers of the left hand, which also began after the fall. She feels that this pain is centered at her 
elbow. Today the patient complains of continued neck pain, as well as pain in the left upper arm. Patient notes 
weakness in the left arm and hand, especially on writing and typing. She is left handed. Strength in the right arm is 
normal. She has undergone 3 sessions of physical therapy to the left shoulder, from which she notes no relief of her 
pain. She notes some difficulties with balance. She experienced some bladder incontinence immediately following the 
injury, but states that bladder function has returned to normal. She has seen a neurologist regarding persistent 
headaches since her fall. An EMG performed on 12/13/19 shows evidence of mild chronic radiculopathy of C5 on the 
left. A cervical MRI scan performed recently shows evidence of small disc bulge at C3-4, slightly distorting the spinal 
cord at that level. I have discussed the pros and cons of surgery as well as the potential risks. These risks include but 
are not limited to infection (3%), hoarseness (5% that resolves spontaneously in 4%), weakness and numbness of the 
arms and legs up to and including permanent quadriplegia, pseudarthrosis, failure to relieve symptoms, as well as the 
risks of anesthesia (pneumonia, myocardial infarction and heart failure, stroke, and death). The patient understands 
these risks and wished to proceed with surgery. 
The patient has the following comorbidities:
Patient Active Problem List
Diagnosis Date Noted
• Herniation of cervical intervertebral disc with radiculopathy [DOB-3560]
• Sprain of shoulder, left [DOB-3559]

After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit NortonHealthcare.com/
eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record for
their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

January 13, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel 
free to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff 
member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

BHMG Orthopaedic Surgery Clinic Note
BAPTIST HEALTH
Subjective
Sismears Wa
CC: Follow-up (8 week follow up Acute pain of left shoulder )
HPI
Amy Mills is a 36 y.o. female. Patient returns the office today for follow-up of her left shoulder pain. In the interim, she
has seen a neurologist and been recommended to have ACDF surgery at C3-4. She is scheduled to have this done
with Dr. Shields in Louisville. She has also seen Dr. Tibbs at UK initially who also recommended surgery.
ROS:
Constiutional:Pt denies fever, chills, nausea, or vomiting.
MSK:as above
Objective
Objective
Past Medical History
Past Medical History:
Diagnosis Date
+ Abnormal Pap smear of cervix
* Urinary incontinence
+ Uterus prolapse
Physical Exam
Pulse 94 | Ht 165.1 cm (65") | Wt 74.4 kg (164 Ib) | LMP [DOB-3500] | SpO2 99% |BMI 27.29 kg/m?
Body mass index is 27.29 kg/m?.

ea GROUP ORTHOPEDICS AND MRN: [PHONE-2312], DOB: [DOB-5810], Sex: F

[DOB-3498] - Office Visit in BAPTIST HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP ORTHOPEDICS AND SPORTS MEDICINE
(continued)
Clinical Notes (continued)
Patient is well nourished and well developed.
Ortho Exam
Passive forward elevation 130
External rotation 60
Weakness with deltoid, bicep, and tricep testing
Imaging/Labs/EMG Reviewed:
Imaging Results (Last 24 Hours)
**No results found for the last 24 hours. **
We reviewed nerve studies brought in with her from Louisville read by Dr. lyer that show a chronic cervical
radiculopathy involving predominantly C5 on the left and incidental finding of carpal tunnel syndrome that is primarily
sensory.
Assessment
Assessment:
1. Acute pain of left shoulder
2. Cervical radiculopathy
3. Central stenosis of spinal canal
4. Adhesive capsulitis of left shoulder
Plan:
1. Recommend over the counter anti-inflammatories for pain and/or swelling
2. Adhesive capsulitis left shoulder with chronic left shoulder pain--patient seems improved from her capsulitis
overall. There is still significant weakness in the shoulder and a burning sensation over the axillary nerve
distribution. Again, presentation like this for any type of organic shoulder issue would be highly atypical and
unlikely. We have shared that with her today.
3. C5 radiculopathy in the face of C3-4 >C4-5 cervical neuroforaminal stenosis--recommended she address the
discrepancy between the planned surgery according to the patient and the new finding of a C5 radiculopathy on
her nerve studies with whichever neurosurgeon she chooses to go with. She will potentially need surgery to
address both the C3-4 and C4-5 issues.
4. Follow-up with me when necessary.
Janak Ramesh Talwalkar, MD

Discharge instructions (specify)
The following discharge instructions were discussed with the
patient and they verbalized understanding
When you go home you may get up and be active
You may go up and down stairs, but make sure to hold on to
the rail and have someone with you
You can ride in a car, but cannot drive until you have been
cleared by your surgeon
You can take the dressing off of your incision on  Friday and
may begin showering on Saturday.
Check your incision daily.  Notify our office if you notice
redness, swelling or drainage from your incision or if you
have fever over 100.5
Keep your head elevated on 4-5 pillows for at least a weekDO NOT LAY FLAT!!!
Stick with soft foods that are easy to swallow.  Take small
bites and chew your food well.
Ice and popsicles help with sore throat
You may not lift anything over 5 pounds until cleared by your
surgeon and do not do any kind of work over your head
Absolutely no nicotine products
Absolutely no Advil, Aleve, Ibuprofen or any other kind of
anti-inflammatory
You cannot take Tylenol with your pain medicine.  Your pain
medicine has Tylenol in it.  You cannot take both
One of the side effects of the pain medication is constipation.
 You will probably need to take an over the counter laxative in
addition to your stool softner.  Please let our office know if
you are having problems having a bowel movement
One of the side effects of your muscle relaxer is drowsiness.
 If you begin to feel too drowsy that you cannot get up and
safely ambulate, decrease the frequency of your muscle
relaxer.
Call our office at [PHONE-2333]  if you have any problems.
 There is someone on-call 24 hours a day
Follow up appointment with neurosurgery on 2/20/20 at
11:40 am with Stephanie Tichenor Nurse Practitioner.

Your providers may have already given you your prescription for pain medication prior to your discharge date. If this is
the case, it is best to be prepared and have your medication filled and ready for your return home from the hospital. You
may want to have your pain pills with you on your ride home if you are traveling a long distance.  • Use medication only as directed. • Remember medication takes time to work.  Most oral pain relievers need at least 20 minutes to take effect.

Amy J. Mills (MRN: EP02256299) (CSN: 10080087152) • Printed at 1/21/20 10:33 AM Page 5 of 6   
Medication Information (continued)
• Remember to take your pain medication before activity and at bedtime. Your provider may advise you to take
your pain medication at regular intervals (such as every 4 to 6 hours). • Be sure to get enough rest. If you are having trouble sleeping, talk to your provider. • Use pillows to support you when you sleep and when you do your coughing and deep breathing exercises • Try using the alternative methods such as heating pads or cold therapy, walking, guided imagery tapes,
listening to soft music, changing your position in bed, aromatherapy, and massage can help relieve your pain.
Safe medication storage and disposal
Medications should be stored by patients in secure locations and excess pills should be disposed of properly, to 
minimize the risk of diversion and inappropriate use.  To find a prescription drug disposal location near you please 
visit https://odcp.ky.gov

Norton Care Call) • You will be asked a few questions about your health and progress. Please answer the questions using the phone
keypad. • Based on your answers, a Norton Healthcare team member may call you to offer help and other instructions.

Hospital Course: Patient was admitted following elective C3-C4 ACDF by Dr. Shields and Dr. 
Johnson, which she tolerated the procedure well. she reported resolution of her preoperative 
neck and arm pain and felt her strength was improved postop. she had normal strength and 
sensation and neck was supple without hematoma. Patient was tolerating eating and 
ambulating without difficulty and was discharged home in stable condition.

Discharge Exam: 
Mental Status: The patient is awake, alert, oriented to person, place, and time. There is no 
dysarthria. Recent and remote memory appears normal for age. Fund of knowledge appears 
normal for age. 
Cranial Nerves: Pupils were equal, round, and reactive to light bilaterally and consensually. 
EOMI without nystagmus. Visual fields were full to confrontation. Face was symmetric. There 
was intact light touch to V1, V2, V3. Hearing intact bilaterally. Palate elevates symmetrically. 
Mm.Qr
Shoulder shrug was normal. Tongue protruded midline. 
: Tone was normal in all four extremities without fasciculations, atrophy, or myoclonus. 
There were no abnormal movements. Strength was 5/5 in bilateral hand intrinsics, bicep, 
triceps, deltoid. Strength is 5/5 in bilateral iliopsoas, quadriceps, anterior tibialis, extensor 
hallucis longer, and gastrocnemius. 
Reflexes: The reflexes are 2/4 for biceps, triceps, brachioradialis, patellar and achillies tendon 
bilaterally and symmetrically. 
Sensorv.: Intact to light touch throughout all dermatones. 
Cerebellar: There was no dysmetria. Fine motor coordination was intact. 
�: Gait was normal based without ataxia. Tandem, heel, and toe gait were intact. 
Incision: clean and dry no redness or swelling

01/24/20 1443 Registration Completed STORMS, MAKAYLA -- 01/24/20 1609 Patient discharged CARPENTER, ASHLEY -- 01/24/20 1609 ED Tracking End CARPENTER, ASHLEY -- ED Provider Note
Subjective
Patient is a 36-year-old female who presents the emergency department for bilateral leg swelling. The patient had 
surgery on her cervical spine on Monday, at Norton Hospital. She had a spinal fusion at the C3-C4 level. The patient 
states that she has had pain with her surgery and has been recovering otherwise well. She does state that her leg 
swelling started a couple days ago. She is not had any pain in the back of her calves or her legs at all but states that 
they have never been swollen like this before. The patient denies any recent fever, chills, nausea, vomiting, shortness 
of breath or dysuria. She has no additional symptoms or complaints at this time. She states that she told her surgeon 
about the symptoms and was sent to the emergency department to rule out a DVT.
Review of Systems 
Constitutional: Negative for activity change, appetite change, chills, diaphoresis, fatigue and fever. 
HENT: Negative for congestion, postnasal drip, rhinorrhea, sinus pressure, sinus pain, sneezing, sore throat and 
tinnitus. 
Eyes: Negative for photophobia, pain, discharge, redness and itching. 
Respiratory: Negative for apnea, cough, choking, chest tightness, shortness of breath, wheezing and stridor. 
Cardiovascular: Positive for leg swelling. Negative for chest pain and palpitations. 
Gastrointestinal: Negative for abdominal distention, abdominal pain, constipation, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting. 
Genitourinary: Negative for difficulty urinating and flank pain. 
Musculoskeletal: Negative for arthralgias and back pain. 
Neurological: Negative for dizziness, facial asymmetry, light-headedness and headaches. 
Psychiatric/Behavioral: Negative for agitation, behavioral problems, confusion and decreased concentration.

Narrative:
Among patients with dyspnea, NT-proBNP is highly sensitive for the detection of acute congestive heart failure. In addition NTproBNP of <300 pg/ml effectively rules out acute congestive heart failure with 99% negative predictive value.
Results may be falsely decreased if patient taking Biotin.

Narrative:
Troponin T Reference Range:
<= 0.03 ng/mL- Negative for AMI
>0.03 ng/mL- Abnormal for myocardial necrosis. Clinicians would have to utilize clinical acumen, EKG, Troponin and serial 
changes to determine if it is an Acute Myocardial Infarction or myocardial injury due to an underlying chronic condition. 
Results may be falsely decreased if patient taking Biotin.

Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
EXAMINATION: US VENOUS DOPPLER LOWER EXTREMITY BILATERAL (DUPLEX)- 
CLINICAL INDICATION: recent surgery, bl leg swelling
TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar gray scale and Doppler vascular sonographic
imaging of the deep veins of BILATERAL lower extremity, without and with
compression. 
COMPARISON: NONE 
FINDINGS: 
Deep Veins: The visualized deep veins demonstrate flow and are
compressible. No evidence of deep venous thrombosis. 
Superficial veins: Unremarkable. Saphenofemoral junction is patent
without thrombus.
Soft tissues: Unremarkable.
Impression:
No DVT.
This report was finalized on 1/24/2020 3:31 PM by Dr. Kevin J Croce, MD.

Our
LSee
mission is to provide you with high quality individualized care that
A
is safe and effective to meet your
healthcare needs. We will provide you with education and instruction to help with your pain
management/recovery, and we will strive for this to be a successful process. This needs to be a team effort to
include not only your care providers at the hospital, but you, the patient.
Our care goals are:
e To work together in providing your treatment in a respectful manner.
e To help you meet the goals of your treatment plan.
e To maintain a safe environment for the delivery of health care services at our hospital.
e To protect the safety and well-being of all patients, visitors, staff and care providers.
We value you as a patient and want to continue to provide you with high quality safe care and service. To do so,
we need to outline the expectations that will foster an effective provider- patient relationship.
The following expectations are required to maintain an effective provider- patient relationship.
Patient Behavior Expectations
e | will comply with the medical treatment plan and recommendations developed between my provider
and me.
e | will treat my care providers with dignity and respect, and | expect to be treated with dignity and respect.
e | understand that profanity, physical or verbal abuse, or any other form of violence against anyone will
not be allowed or tolerated.
e | understand that unauthorized and/or illegal drugs are not allowed in the hospital.
e For my safety, | will not leave the nursing unit where | am a patient during my treatment unless
accompanied by hospital personnel for diagnostic procedures or treatments.
e If leave the unit, it will be considered going against medical advice and | may be discharged from the
hospital. For any future medical care, | would need to consult my primary care MD/Provider or go to an
Emergency Room for further evaluation. Hospital staff have the right not to re-admit me for further care
unless an emergency situation exists.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS saoaiisam 0oHTAOVO(TITT1OUI TTING
. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my admitting and
treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. In the case of diagnostic studies, | consent to the interpretation of the studies at an
off-site location through the use of telehealth. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my
care under the supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical,
medical, and/or diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing
for communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no guarantees
about the results that may be obtained from my care.
. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and
certified registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
. CONSENT TO USE AND DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION: | authorize the Hospital and the health care providers on its medical staff
to use and disclose information about me to carry out treatment, payment and healthcare operations. Examples of such uses and
disclosures include, but are not limited to, providing information to: (a) any person, company, or entity (such as HMOs, insurance
companies, workers’ compensation carriers, my employer, the Medicare and Medicaid programs and their intermediaries and review
organizations, and any other payor or its review organization or third party administrator) that is or may be liable for paying any claim for
benefits arising out of services provided to me on an inpatient or outpatient basis, or for any ambulance services provided for my transport
to or from the Hospital; (b) any health care provider or ambulance company providing services for me; (c) any providers who may be
providing follow-up care to me after discharge such as home health agencies, nursing homes, physicians, and other health care facilities to
which the patient may be transferred; (d) any licensing or accrediting organizations necessary for the Hospital to obtain or maintain
licensure or accreditation; (e) to providers or entities for the purposes of conducting medical audits, utilization reviews, or quality assurance
reviews; and (f) any other persons or entities described in the Hospital’s Notice of Privacy Practices. This consent includes the release of
medical records and billing information related to drug-related conditions, alcoholism, psychological and psychiatric conditions, and/or
communicable or blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). | also
consent to any third party payor or its review organization paying for my care to discuss my plan of treatment for utilization review purposes. |
also authorize the release of my social security number to the manufacturer of any implanted medical device in accordance with the Medical
Device Tracking Act.
. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to other
arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering services to
me. | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party
payor regardless of the reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered
services or medically necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for
payment under Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be
made on my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability carrier or,
any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment. This assignment
shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions necessary to assist
Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any unpaid account may be turned
over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to pursue collection, | agree to pay all
reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in collecting my account. Pursuant to the
Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize SearchAmerica, or any other a credit reporting agency, to release to the
Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. | understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to
obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or

5. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. According to Medicare
regulations, | acknowledge that | am responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a
prescription drug. These drugs are also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel.
Medicare requires Hospital to bill Medicare patients or other third party payers for these drugs. Medicare Part D beneficiaries may submit a
paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment
materials.
6. HOSPITAL IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR LOSS OF PERSONAL ARTICLES: | UNDERSTAND THAT THE HOSPITAL MAINTAINS A
SAFE FOR THE SAFEKEEPINGOF MONEY AND VALUABLES AND THAT THE HOSPITAL IS NOT LIABLE FORTHE LOSS OF OR DAMAGE
TO MONEY, JEWELRY,GLASSES, DENTURES, DOCUMENTS, CLOTHING, OR OTHER ITEMS OF PERSONAL PROPERTY.
7. SMOKE-FREE ENVIRONMENT: This Hospital maintains a smoke-free environment on its campus. Smoking is prohibited by health care
personnel, patients, and visitors on the Hospital’s campus. | understand that | am to remain in my treatment area at all times. | further
understand that if | leave my treatment area, | will not be under nursing or medical supervision, and further understand that leaving my
treatment area may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.
8. NOTICE OF NONDISCRIMINATION: Hospital programs and activities are available regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex
(including gender identity and gender stereotyping), disability or any other status protected by federal, state or local law.
9. AIDS INFORMATION:| have received a copy of the AIDS information sheet.
10. LATEX ALLERGIES:| havealatex allergy Yes
11. PATIENT RIGHTS AND ADVANCE DIRECTIVES:

[DOB-2498] - ED in Baptist Health Corbin Emergency Department (continued)
Edema is when you have too much fluid in your body or under your skin. Edema may 
make your legs, feet, and ankles swell up. Swelling is also common in looser tissues, 
like around your eyes. This is a common condition. It gets more common as you get 
older. There are many possible causes of edema. Eating too much salt (sodium) and 
being on your feet or sitting for a long time can cause edema in your legs, feet, and 
ankles. Hot weather may make edema worse.
Edema is usually painless. Your skin may look swollen or shiny.
Follow these instructions at home: · Keep the swollen body part raised (elevated) above the level of your heart when you are sitting or lying down. · Do not sit still or stand for a long time. · Do not wear tight clothes. Do not wear garters on your upper legs. · Exercise your legs. This can help the swelling go down. · Wear elastic bandages or support stockings as told by your doctor. · Eat a low-salt (low-sodium) diet to reduce fluid as told by your doctor. · Depending on the cause of your swelling, you may need to limit how much fluid you drink (fluid restriction). · Take over-the-counter and prescription medicines only as told by your doctor.
Contact a doctor if: · Treatment is not working. · You have heart, liver, or kidney disease and have symptoms of edema. · You have sudden and unexplained weight gain.
Get help right away if: · You have shortness of breath or chest pain. · You cannot breathe when you lie down. · You have pain, redness, or warmth in the swollen areas. · You have heart, liver, or kidney disease and get edema all of a sudden. · You have a fever and your symptoms get worse all of a sudden.
Summary
· Edema is when you have too much fluid in your body or under your skin. · Edema may make your legs, feet, and ankles swell up. Swelling is also common in looser tissues, like around 
your eyes. · Raise (elevate) the swollen body part above the level of your heart when you are sitting or lying down. · Follow your doctor's instructions about diet and how much fluid you can drink (fluid restriction).
This information is not intended to replace advice given to you by your health care provider. Make sure you discuss 
any questions you have with your health care provider.
More Information

Elsevier Interactive Patient Education © 2019 Elsevier Inc. · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding · Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get 
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a 
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you 
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor

After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

February 20, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel 
free to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff 
member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.

Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor

Real people. Remarkable care.
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

February 20, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel 
free to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff 
member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

Messages (continued)
May 26, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel 
free to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff 
member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Subjective:
Subjective Patient ID: Amy Mills is a 36 yr/o female. Amy is a 36 yr/o old female who is seen today for follow up evaluation of neck pain. She is now 4 months s/p C3-4
anterior cervical discectomy and fusion with plate secondary to cervical stenosis. She states that she has been doing
very well post-operatively. She notes that her pre-operative neck pain has completely resolved. She does complain of
some right shoulder pain, but this is unrelated to her cervical surgery. She denies any pain, numbness, or weakness
involving the upper extremities. She is satisfied with the overall surgical outcome. The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate: allergies, current
medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past surgical history and problem list. Review of Systems
Constitutional: Negative for activity change, appetite change, fever and unexpected weight change. HENT: Negative for tinnitus, trouble swallowing and voice change. Respiratory: Negative for shortness of breath and wheezing. Cardiovascular: Negative for chest pain and palpitations. Gastrointestinal: Negative for constipation, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting. Genitourinary: Negative for difficulty urinating, dysuria, frequency and urgency. Musculoskeletal: Positive for arthralgias. Negative for back pain, gait problem, myalgias, neck pain and neck stiffness.

Skin: Negative for rash and wound. Allergic/Immunologic: Negative for environmental allergies, food allergies and immunocompromised state. Neurological: Negative for dizziness, weakness, numbness and headaches. Hematological: Negative for adenopathy. Does not bruise/bleed easily. All other systems reviewed and are negative. Objective:
Objective
Ht 5' 3" (160 cm) | Wt 78 kg (172 lb) | BMI 30.47 kg/m² Physical Exam
Constitutional: She is oriented to person, place, and time. She appears well-developed and well-nourished. No
distress. 
HENT: 
Head: Normocephalic and atraumatic. Eyes: Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light. Neck: Normal range of motion. Pulmonary/Chest: Effort normal. No respiratory distress. Musculoskeletal: 
 Comments: On physical examination, she is oriented x3 and is a very good historian. She is an independent
ambulator with a normal gait. She has a fairly normal appearing cervical lordosis. She is neurologically intact
in both upper extremities with respect sensation and motor. Skin integuments are normal and there are no
palpable masses. Extraocular movements are full, facial expressions are symmetrical, respirations are not
labored. 
Neurological: She is alert and oriented to person, place, and time. Skin: Skin is warm and dry. She is not diaphoretic. Psychiatric: She has a normal mood and affect. Her behavior is normal. Vitals reviewed.
Assessment and Plan:
Assessment
Amy was seen today for neck pain. Diagnoses and all orders for this visit:
Cervical stenosis of spinal canal
S/P cervical spinal fusion
I have spent 25 minutes in consultation with the patient today. I have also spent time extensively reviewing imaging
with the patient. Her x-ray showed the instrumentation at C3-4 to be in good position. Based on her symptoms and
radiology, I believe that the patient is doing very well post-operatively. She does not report any significant neck pain at
this time. In regards to her shoulder pain, I have advised the patient to be seen by a general orthopaedic surgeon for

Real people. Remarkable care.
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and howto schedule an eCare appointment, visit NortonHealthcare.com/
eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record for
their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

8/7/2020 10:11 PM by Amy Mills
May 26, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel
free to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff 
member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

[DOB-2897] - Office Visit in Norton Orthopedic Institute (continued)
Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Subjective:
Subjective Amy Mills is a 36 yr/o female new patient referred by Dr. Shields seen today for Shoulder Pain (new patient 
visit regarding left shoulder pain)
. Symptoms involve the left shoulder. Symptoms have been present for at least 2 years. Symptoms began after a 
slip and fall. During the same fall she also injured her cervical spine. Her cervical spine injury caused the left upper 
extremity symptoms. When she had an ACDF she said that most of her symptoms were immediately resolved. 
However she has had persistent left shoulder pain. Pain is diffuse but mostly superior. The pain is described as 
burning stabbing and severe. Symptoms are present most of the time but are exacerbated by activity.
Review of Systems 
Musculoskeletal: Positive for joint pain and neck pain. Negative for back pain, falls and myalgias. 
 LEFT SHOULDER 
Neurological: Positive for weakness. Negative for dizziness, tingling, tremors, sensory change, speech change, focal 
weakness, seizures, loss of consciousness and headaches. 
 Radiating pains
Addendum Note

Clinical Notes (continued)
She has seen a couple of other orthopedic surgeons and continues to have no answers or relief. She had an outside 
noncontrast MRI performed which was nondiagnostic
I have carefully explained to them that although most of the symptoms from her cervical spine injury are resolved it is 
possible that there is residual nerve damage.
Recommend MRI arthrogram for a more sensitive shoulder advanced imaging
She can call for results 
Imaging

Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for afever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention ofyour
doctor

After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and youwill be routed to an on-call provider.
MEDICALRECORDS
To request a copy ofyour medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at httpsi//medicalrecordrequest nortonhealthcare.org
‘Once received, your request will be processed within 7business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours aday, seven days aweek through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: avideo visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

June 18, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and 
then think of non-urgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel 
free to use the attached questionnaire to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff 
member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we 
hope you will take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your 
feedback to improve our patient care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare 
emergency department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

[DOB-2993] - FL lou contrast injection ct mri in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN XRAY (continued)
Imaging (continued)
FL CONTRAST INJECTION CT/MRICLINICAL INDICATION: Left shoulder pain; M25.512-Pain in left shoulder
COMPARISON: None available.
Total fluoroscopy time 2.08 minutes.
FINDINGS: Preliminary images show no fracture or dislocation.
Preservation of the joint space.
The procedure and possible complications were discussed with the
patient. She stated that she understood and wished to continue.
Fluoroscopy was used to localize a site for entry. Site was prepped and
draped in the usual sterile fashion. Topical anesthesia was provided
with lidocaine. A 22-gauge needle was then inserted into the left
glenohumeral joint. A mixture of bupivacaine, iodinated contrast, and
gadolinium as well as sterile saline was injected into the joint space.
Some of the injection was extraarticular.
No evidence of a tear of the supraspinatus tendon on the study.
Patient was asked to exercise her shoulder and subsequently was taken to
MRI.
Impression:
Technically successful left shoulder arthrogram. No tear
seen on this portion of the exam. MRI is pending. 
This report was finalized on 6/24/2020 11:42 AM by Dr. Harold Reedy, MD.

| understand that many professional services are provided by independent medical doctors and other advanced care practitioners
who practice at Baptist Health and that they are not agents or employees of Baptist Health and are considered independent
contractors. These independent contractors may include, but are not limited to, anesthesiologists, certified registered nurse
anesthetists, pathologists, radiologists, emergency room providers and other medical specialists. | understand that | will be billed
separately for provider services and at rates established by the providers.
The provider has explained to me: (1) the nature of the proposed procedure; (2) the potential benefits, risks, or side effects, including
potential problems that might occur during recuperation; (3) the likelihood of achieving goals; (4) reasonable alternatives to the
procedure, if any; and (5) the relevant benefits, risks, and side effects associated with those alternatives, including the possible
results of not receiving care, treatment, and services. Based on the provider's explanation of the benefits and tisks of this procedure
and the alternatives available, | agree that the potential benefits of the procedure(s) outweigh the potential risks involved. The
provider has explained to me that all surgical procedures involve some risk, including risks such as severe loss of blood, infection,
cardiac arrest and, in rare instances, death. | acknowledge that medicine is not an exact science and that no guarantees have been
made to me concerning the results of the procedure(s) described above. | understand that if complications occur, additional medical
treatment may be required and emergency medical treatment is available.
ADDITIONAL PROCEDURES
| consent to the performance of various procedures in adcition to, or different from, those contemplated, which are related to the
above procedure and which the herein above-designated provider and/or his/her assistants or associates may consider necessary or
advisable during the course of the procedure.
I CT: NA
If you wish to not receive blood or blood products please request to sign a refusal form. 1) Some surgical procedures may require
blood transfusions or blood products. | was informed that | may need to have blood products administered during the course of my
treatment. 2) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products involves the introduction of the product into one
of my veins, commonly in the arm using a needle. | understand that the amount of blood products administered is determined by my
provider based upon my particular needs. 3) It has been explained to me that the administration of blood products may expose me to
certain risks. Minor or temporary reactions, such as slight bruising, swelling, or local infection of the area where the needle pierces
the skin is not uncommon. Non-serious reactions may include headache, fever, or a mild skin reaction, such as itching or a rash.
Rare, but serious reactions are possible such as the transmission of diseases including hepatitis and AIDS, which require lengthy
treatment and can be fatal. It has been further explained that while procedures for screening blood products for diseases such as
AIDS and hepatitis are effective, these procedures are not perfect and there is a risk of exposure to AIDS, hepatitis, or other bloodborne disease. |have taken all of these risks into consideration and have decided that the benefits of receiving blood products
outweigh the risks. 4) | have been made aware of the reasonably available alternatives to the administration of blood products and
the risks and benefits of those alternatives including autologous blood transfusion, directed donation and not having any blood
products administered. | understand that | may be able to donate one of several units of my own blood to be used during planned
elective surgery and that my provider must determine my ability to donate for myself. This is called autologous transfusion. If 1
choose to have an autologous transfusion, | understand that the last unit of blood must be donated one week prior to expected use.
| also understand that | may choosea direct donation. A direct donation means | am allowed to select my own donors. There is no
scientific evidence that directed donations are safer but | understand many patients feel more comfortable designating their own
donors. The designated donor can donate within thirty (30) days of the anticipated need for the blood. It takes at least seventy-two
(72) hours for a unit from a directed donor to be processed and available for transfusion. These arrangements must be made by my
provider. Additionally, my procedure(s) may require the services of a perfusionist; a perfusionist assists in the management of the
lungs anc blood flow during heart surgery.

OPERATIVE / PROCEDURAL PERMIT
(Anesthesia & Related Procedures)
MEDIA REPRESENTATION
I consent to the taking of photographs, motion pictures, or videotapes during this procedure(s}. | authorize cameramen,
photographers, technicians, and equipment designed for such purposes to be admitted to the operating room. | understand that
the photographs, motion pictures, or videotapes may become part of my medical record and may be used for scientific and/or
educational purposes as well as for my continued medical care. Provided, however, inthe event such is used for scientific and/or
educational purposes, my name will not be used and| will not be otherwise identified. | release Baptist Health, its agents,
servants, employees, consultants, and providers from any liability in connection with the use of the photographs, pictures, or
videotapes.
TISSUE REMOVAL
Tunderstand that this procedure(s) may result in the removal of body tissue or body parts. | consent to their disposal according to
the hospital policies and the recommendations of the provider.
VENDOR REPRESENTATIVES / OBSERVERS
l understand that there may be a representative from a company that furnishes equipment to providers and hospitals present in
the room during my surgical procedure. | also understand that his/her function is strictly to observe and advise, and that this
person has no part in my direct care. | have been informed and understand that, when specifically requested by the provider, a
limited and specified number of individuals (excluding parents and blood relatives) are permitted to observe an operation or
procedure. | hereby consent to allow those individuals, specifically authorized by the provider, to observe the operation or
procedure consented to above.
CLINICAL STUDENTS
Baptist Health participates in clinical education programs with colleges and universities to give students, including but not limited
to, nursing students, medical students, interns, and residents (“students”), experience in clinical practice. Your provider may
permit students to observe and participate in your care under the provider's direct supervision.
ADVANCE DIRECTIVE
acknowledge and agree that any advance directive including, but not limited to, a do not resuscitate order, a medical order for
scope of treatment or an advance directive which outline my desire for a level of treatment other than full treatment will be
temporarily suspended during the procedure unless otherwise requested by me and documented by my physician in the medical
record.
ANESTHESIA
understand that this procedure may require anesthesia. An anesthesiology provider has or will explain the potential benefits,
tisks, side effects, and potential anesthesia alternatives. If your provider recommends and will manage local anesthesia or
moderate sedation as noted below, this discussion will occur with your provider. Complications from any type of anesthetic are
infrequent, but do occur. Complications are more frequent for patients in poor health than for patients in good health.
Complications associated with all types of anesthesia include temporary or permanent damage to teeth, lips, nose, throat, voice
box, windpipe, eyes, nerves, or blood vessels. Drug reaction may occur and may be minor or serious even requiring
resuscitation. Breathing problems may develop. Headache, back pain, some degree of awareness, infection, nausea and/or
vomiting may occur. There are some very serious complications, which are uncommon, but can occur. These include paralysis,
brain damage, or death. There are other potential complications depending on your situation, however, itis not possible to advise
you of every imaginable complication. Serious complications are unlikely. The benefits of surgery and anesthesia, except in
extreme circumstances, outweigh the potential risks. |agree that the potential benefit of the procedure(s) outweighs the potential
risks of anesthesia.
| understand that my procedure is scheduled to be done with one or more of the following:
General Anesthesia Monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist, a Certified Registered Nurse
Anesthetist [CRNA], or an Anesthesiologist's Assistant [AA].
Monitored Anesthesia Care Possible sedation, monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist, a CRNA, or
an AA.
Regional Anesthesia Monitoring and care provided by the anesthesiologist, CRNA, or an AA.
Local Anesthesia Care managed by the Provider and monitored by a registered nurse.
Moderate Sedation Care managed by the Provider and monitored by a registered nurse.

| understand that MRI Scan may require the injection of contrast (a drug) into my bloodstream to assist in
visualization of certain areas in my body. A smal! number of patients may be allergic to contrast and occasionally
experience these reactions: headache, nausea, or coldness at the injection site. Very, very, rarely, other adverse
reactions are possible such as chest pain, rash, impaired breathing, or death. There is no means of testing you for
an allergic reaction before injecting the contrast. | understand that if complications occur, additional medica!
treatment may be required and emergency medica! treatment is available.
| If contrast is to be used in performing your MRI, please answer the following questions.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91082876279 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. | further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, | understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom it does not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.
4. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that | am
responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a prescription drug. These drugs are
also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel. Hospital may bill Medicare patients or

CONSENT TO EMAIL OR TEXT USAGE FOR HEALTHCARE COMMUNICATIONS: If at any time | provide an email address at which |

· M25.512 (ICD-10-CM) - Left shoulder pain
PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding · Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get

MRI Shoulder Left Arthrogram [272862387] Resulted: 06/24/20 1336, Result status: Final result
Ordering provider: Khalily, Cyna, MD 06/24/20 0919 Order status: Completed
Resulted by: Reedy, Harold W. Jr., MD Filed by: Interface, Rad Results Enterprise In 06/24/20 1338
Performed: 06/24/20 1114 - 06/24/20 1314 Accession number: [PHONE-3106]
Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
MRI SHOULDER LEFT ARTHROGRAM 
CLINICAL INDICATION: M25.512; M25.512-Pain in left shoulder;
G89.29-Other chronic pain. 
COMPARISON: [DOB-3107]
PROCEDURE: Multiple planar images of the left shoulder were acquired in
a high field strength magnet. Several pulse sequences were used to
acquire the study. IV gadolinium was not administered. However, the
patient was injected intra-articular prior to the exam.
FINDINGS: A small portion of the injection was extra-articular, thus
accounting for fluid in the adjacent musculature.
There is no evidence of a tear of the rotator cuff.
There is signal in the superior labrum, concerning for an anterior
superior labral tear.
No adjacent soft tissue mass is seen.
No acute fracture.
Impression:
1. Partial extra-articular injection.
2. There is signal within the superior labrum anteriorly concerning for
a small anterior labral tear.
3. No evidence of a rotator cuff tear. 
This report was finalized on 6/24/2020 1:36 PM by Dr. Harold Reedy, MD.

Please click epichttp://appointments[here] to view more details about your appointment and

You can use eCheck-In to update your information online before you arrive.
Your safety is our first priority at Baptist Health. If you are experiencing symptoms you feel 
may be related to the COVID-19 virus such as fever greater than 100, acute respiratory 
symptoms (cough and/or shortness of air) or a sudden loss of smell or taste or do not feel 
comfortable coming into an office setting, you may access e-visit and video visit options 
through Baptist Health MyChart. For the health and safety of our patients, all patients and 
visitors will be asked to arrive wearing a mask. Visitors are not permitted with the exception of 
patients under age 18 - one parent may accompany the patient. Dependent patients who 
require assistance and hospice or end-of-life patients may bring one caregiver. All visitors will 
be screened, which may include having a temperature taken. Please check with your provider 
if you have questions. Thank you for trusting Baptist Health with your care!
To schedule a telemedicine appointment, you can go epichttp://evisit[here] to complete an evisit questionnaire online, or go epichttp://apptmake[here] to schedule a video visit.

CONTRAINDICATIONS: PATIENT MUST BE OFF ANY MEDICATIONS THAT CONTAIN GLUCOPHAGE
(METFORMIN), FOR 48 HOURS AFTER ANY INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST EXAMINATION. CHECK WITH
YOUR PHYSICIAN OR PHARMACIST IF UNSURE IF YOUR MEDICATIONS CONTAIN THESE DRUGS.
Your doctor has scheduled you for an x-ray examination that requires an injection of contrast into your blood stream. The
contrast provides the radiologist (A medical doctor who specializes in x-ray procedures and diagnosis) more information than
with x-ray done without the contrast. The contrast may make you feel slightly warm or flushed for a few seconds. This feeling
usually goes away quickly. As with any drug, some patients may be allergic to the contrast. The most common allergic
reaction is itching and hives. Occasionally, (Approx. 1 in 1,000) a patient will develop a more serious reaction, such as
impaired breathing or heart beat irregularities. These reactions may require treatment, which is immediately available in the xray department. Very rarely, (Approx. 1 in 120,000 or less) the contrast reaction can be so severe that death results. There is
no means of testing you for an allergic reaction before injecting the contrast.
