1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. | further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, | understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom it does not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.
4. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that | am
responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a prescription drug. These drugs are
also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel. Hospital may bill Medicare patients or

PREVENTING SURGICAL SITE INFECTIONS
· Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding · Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause

It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get 
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a 
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you 
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/

Instructions for After Discharge
Patient Instructions
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding · Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get 
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a 
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org

VISIT TYPE: Office Visit
This 36 year old female presents for MA Notes, HPI, Neck Pain and Left Shoulder Pain.
History of Present Illness:
1. MA Notes 
Pt presents today for a telephone visit for a f/u
PHQ-2 Moderately severe depression
UDS ordered
Pt is due for UDS, med agt, tdap
 Audio visit only, no capability for Audio/Visual visit at this time. Verbal consent for treatment via
telephone was obtained after informing the patient of the risks and benefits.
2. HPI 
Patient initiated call. Obtained verbal consent from the patient to proceed.
Follow up instructions given
I spent minutes on the telephone call with the patient
3. Neck Pain 
The severity of the problem is moderate. The problem has worsened. The frequency of pain is daily. 
Location of pain is bilateral posterior neck. The patient describes the pain as Aching and Throbbing. 
The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom do not include injury. Aggravating factors
include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include joint pain, muscle spasm and
tenderness.
4. Left Shoulder Pain 
It occurs constantly and is worsening. Location: left shoulder. The pain is aching and throbbing. 
Context: there is an injury. The pain is aggravated by movement. Associated symptoms include
decreased mobility, difficulty initiating sleep and joint tenderness. Additional information: MRI
showed labrum tear.

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Subjective:
Subjective Amy Mills is a 36 yr/o female patientseen today for Results (Follow up cisit regarding MRI of left shoulder)
. Shoulder symptoms are unchanged. Symptoms have been present for at least 2 years. Symptoms began after a 
slip and fall. During the same fall she also injured her cervical spine. Her cervical spine injury caused the left upper 
extremity symptoms. When she had an ACDF she said that most of her symptoms were immediately resolved. 
However she has had persistent left shoulder pain. Pain is diffuse but mostly superior. The pain is described as 
burning stabbing and severe. Symptoms are present most of the time but are exacerbated by activity. She also 
describes a feeling of the shoulder being "forward" or feeling "out of place"
Review of Systems 
Musculoskeletal: Positive for joint pain and neck pain. Negative for back pain, falls and myalgias. 
 LEFT SHOULDER 
Neurological: Positive for weakness. Negative for dizziness, tingling, tremors, sensory change, speech change, focal 
weakness, seizures, loss of consciousness and headaches. 
 Radiating pains
 Objective:
Objective Physical Exam
Constitutional: well-developed and well-nourished
Appearance: in no acute distress
Gait: normal
Assistive devices: none
Lymphatics: no palpable axillary adenopathy and no palpable supraclavicular adenopathy

Assessment
1. Superior glenoid labrum lesion of left shoulder, 
subsequent encounter 
2. Pre-operative laboratory examination COVID-19, Novel Coronavirus PAT (PreAdmission Testing): Reason for Testing: Pre 
Admission Testing
Plan:
Plan Patient was allowed to ask questions and stated their satisfaction. 
Discussed diagnosis and treatment options with patient and spouse
MRI is read as a possible anterior labrum defect. This is what I suspected. I have discussed with them that the 
radiologist interpretation is of a "small" tear but again this could be responsible for for her symptoms. Also possible 
that it is not
However it is been over a year and she continues to have significant pain and is willing to undergo shoulder 
arthroscopy. I told her that shoulder arthroscopy with or without labrum repair
The risks, benefits and alternatives of surgery were discussed with the patient including, but not limited to: 
Having a shoulder arthroscopy may put you at risk of having an infection or bleeding. Other parts of the shoulder, such 
as nerves, blood vessels, or ligaments may be damaged. Your shoulder may become stiff, numb, or more painful. 
Medicines, including anesthesia, and devices used during surgery may cause an allergy. Sometimes, the shoulder 
problem may come back after being treated successfully. If left untreated, the pain and problems you have with your 
shoulder may get worse. You may have trouble going back to your usual activities, including sports. Call your

Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, asmall reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for afever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor

Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue Using Norton eCafe through MyNortonChart.

August 6, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and then think of nonurgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel free to use the attached questionnaire 
to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent 
questions you may have. Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we hope you will 
take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your feedback to improve our patient 
care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare emergency 
department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and
some pain is also not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office
for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your
injection site should be brought to the attention of your doctor
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

9/20/23, 4:24 PM MyChart - Visit Summary
https://mychart.nortonhealthcare.org/MyChart/inside.asp?mode=visitsummary&csn=WP-24wDYiROcGQPev45D4vpOngg-3D-3D-24IZ0Ar-2F0tOXjP2… 3/6
What You Need To Know:
You have been given a controlled substance prescription. A controlled substance is a
medicine that is monitored or regulated by the government because they can be
improperly used or abused. People can become addicted to these medicines. Controlled
substance medicines are often used for:
Pain
Sleeping problems
Worry
Anxiety
Cough or colds
There are risks to taking these types of medicines including the possibility of:
Physical drug dependence – You may not be able to stop using the medications.
When you try to stop, you may have withdrawal symptoms and strong cravings
for the medications.
Drug tolerance - When you need to use more medications, or use them more
often, to get the effects you want.
Addiction – When a person uses the controlled substance to get high instead of
using them to control the medical problem. You will have the urge to continue
using the medications even when you know the risks.
Drug overdose - When a person takes more medications than their body can
handle. This may be a small amount or a large amount of medication.
The risks are greatest when the medicine is taken for long periods of time or not taken
correctly.
The goal of treatment is to improve pain, function and help you feel better. These
medications will not completely relieve all your pain or cure your medical problem.
It is important to discuss with your health care provider the goal of treatment with
these medicines. You need to have a plan to stop the medicine as soon as your
medical problem is better.
Safety Measures:
These medicines should only be taken as directed by your healthcare provider.
Keep medicines locked up and out of reach of children.
Do not take medication in front of children as they are likely to mimic your
behavior.
Do not drink alcohol while taking these medicines. Drinking alcohol with a
controlled substance can cause your breathing to slow or stop.
Do not drive while taking these medicines. You can become drowsy and fall
asleep.
Taking a controlled substance while pregnant can be harmful to your baby. Let
your provider know you are pregnant immediately to discuss possible risks.
When medicines are no longer needed, you should safely dispose of the
medicines. Studies have shown that many people who become addicted to these
medications used someone else’s medication found in their home. In Louisville,
medication drop boxes are located at:
Management of Controlled Substance
IN NORTON
HEALTHCARE

-DEA Drug Disposal Location Look Up

RxDisposal=true
If you are unable to access these locations, there are ways to safely throw away
medications at home. Some medicines can be flushed to prevent unintended access by
others.
-Refer to the FDA website to check your specific medicine www.fda.gov
-The Kentucky Poison Control Center (www.kypoisoncontrol.com) can
answer questions. Medical staff are available are available through the
hotline 24 hours a day at 1-[PHONE-3318].
Medication Side Effects:
Breathing too slow or stopped
Drowsiness/tiredness
Dizziness
Constipation
Nausea and vomiting
Itching
Dry mouth
It is important to talk with your healthcare provider about any side effects you experience.
CALL 911 or have a family member call if:
Breathing is slower than normal
You cannot be awakened
You have a seizure
Talk with your healthcare provider about other ways to manage pain:
Apply heat on the area of pain 20-30 minutes every 2 hours as directed to help
decrease pain and muscle spasms.
Consider other Medications. Talk to your healthcare provider about different
medication options.
Go to physical therapy as directed. Physical therapist help teach you exercises
to decrease pain and improve your quality of life.
Exercise for 30 minutes, 3 times a week. Regular physical activity can decrease
pain and improve movement and strength.
Get enough sleep. Create a bedtime routine. Go to sleep and wake up at the
same time every day. Avoid afternoon caffeine.

To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your
MyNortonChart account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at
https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online
options using your MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of
their health record for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and
immunization history.
Talk with a counselor or therapist. Untreated depression or anxiety can worsen
pain symptoms. A type of counseling called cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
can help your pain by changing the way you think about it. CBT can also improve
your mood, sleep, and the way you move.
Follow Up With Your Healthcare Provider:
You will need to go to your provider often when taking these medicines. The
provider will need to check to see how the medicine is working. Make sure you
go to all appointments.
Call your provider if:
Your medical problem has changed
You are having side effects that are concerning to you. Some of
the side effects can be helped by changing the dose of the
medicine.
You have any questions or concerns

May shower when dressing removed

Provide home instruction sheet (specify)
SHOULDER 
* Regular diet at home 
* Leave dressing on and clean for 3 days. 
* May shower when dressing removed if there is no drainage 
from the incisions.  If there   
   is drainage, keep incision clean and covered. 
* Use ice or polar pack on and off for the next 2 - 3 days, no 
more than 20 minutes at a 
   time.   
* Continue to use sling AT ALL TIMES. You may CAREFULLY 
remove to 
   shower ONLY. 
* If you have a nerve block, it will wear off sometime today or 
tonight.  Make sure to start 
   taking pain medicine BEFORE the pain starts. 
* Call off for shortness of breath and signs of infection. 
* If you have pictures from surgery, bring them to your 
follow-up appointment.
Remove dressing
in 3 days

Your providers may have already given you your prescription for pain medication prior to your discharge date. If this is 
the case, it is best to be prepared and have your medication filled and ready for your return home from the hospital. You 
may want to have your pain pills with you on your ride home if you are traveling a long distance. 
Use medication only as directed.
Remember medication takes time to work.  Most oral pain relievers need at least 20 minutes to take effect.
Remember to take your pain medication before activity and at bedtime. Your provider may advise you to take 
your pain medication at regular intervals (such as every 4 to 6 hours).
Be sure to get enough rest. If you are having trouble sleeping, talk to your provider.
Use pillows to support you when you sleep and when you do your coughing and deep breathing exercises 
Try using the alternative methods such as heating pads or cold therapy, walking, guided imagery tapes, 
listening to soft music, changing your position in bed, aromatherapy, and massage can help relieve your pain.
Safe medication storage and disposal
Medications should be stored by patients in secure locations and excess pills should be disposed of properly, to 
minimize the risk of diversion and inappropriate use.  To find a prescription drug disposal location near you please 
visit https://odcp.ky.gov
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record 
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history. 
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication. 
If you are going home after leaving the hospital you will receive a Norton Care Call
Within 48 hours of discharge, you should receive an automated phone call from [PHONE-3324] (Caller ID will read 
Norton Care Call)
You will be asked a few questions about your health and progress. Please answer the questions using the phone 
keypad.
Based on your answers, a Norton Healthcare team member may call you to offer help and other instructions. 
Where to pick up your medications
Pick up these medications at West Knox Pharmacy - Corbin, KY - 14161 North US 25E

Amy Mills (MRN: EP02256299) (CSN: 10091617656) • Printed at 8/10/20  2:37 PM Page 6 of 6   
PATIENT INSTRUCTIONS
POST-ANESTHESIA
IMMEDIATELY FOLLOWING SURGERY: Do not drive, drink any alcohol, operate machinery, make important decisions, 
or sign any legal paperwork for the next twenty four hours after surgery or while taking narcotic pain medications or 
sedatives. You may experience a sore throat, mild nausea, dizziness, drowsiness, or a headache. These symptoms are 
normal after surgery and will subside in the next 24 hours. If you develop severe nausea and vomiting, a severe 
headache, or a high fever please notify your doctor immediately. 
DO NOT TAKE TYLENOL WITH YOUR PERCOCET, DRINK PLENTY OF WATER, AND TAKE WITH FOOD.
CALL DR.KHALILY'S OFFICE IF YOU NOTICE ANY SIGNS OR SYMPTOMS OF INFECTION SUCH AS: INCREASED 
REDNESS, SWELLING, FOUL SMELLING DRAINAGE, FEVER, RED STREAKS, OR CHILLS.

(BMI) of 30.0 to 30.9 in adult (E66.09).
Patient Plan Grains 7-8 daily: such as whole grain breads, cereals, oatmeal and grits. Fruits and
vegetables 4-5 daily. Dairy foods 2-3 per day: nonfat or low fat milk, yogurt and low fat
cheeses. Meats, poultry and fish 2 or fewer servings daily. Nuts, seeds and legumes(

Patient Summary as-of Visit (continued)
Clinical Notes
Subjective:
Subjective Patient ID: Amy Mills is a 36 yr/o female.
Amy is a 36 yr/o old female who is seen today for follow up evaluation of neck pain. She has a history of an anterior 
cervical discectomy and fusion at C3-4 followed by placement of a cervical cage by me and Dr. Shields on 1/20/2020. 
She states that she was doing very well until she underwent shoulder surgery recently. She was seen by Dr. Khalily 
this morning and was advised to be seen in our office today. She describes an episode during her shoulder surgery 
where she jerked while going under anesthesia. She complains of neck pain and soreness. She has not been able to 
wear the sling for her shoulder due to the pain. She has not been seen by Dr. Shields recently. She denies any pain, 
numbness, or tingling involving her upper extremities. 
The following portions of the patient's history were reviewed and updated as appropriate: allergies, current 
medications, past family history, past medical history, past social history, past surgical history and problem list.

Objective

Physical Exam
Constitutional: She is oriented to person, place, and time. She appears well-developed and well-nourished. No 
distress. 
HENT: 
Head: Normocephalic and atraumatic. 
Eyes: Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light. 
Neck: Normal range of motion. 
Pulmonary/Chest: Effort normal. No respiratory distress. 
Musculoskeletal: 
 Comments: On physical examination, she is oriented x3 and is a very good historian. She is an independent 
ambulator with a normal gait. She has a fairly normal appearing cervical lordosis. She is neurologically intact 
in both upper extremities with respect sensation and motor. Skin integuments are normal and there are no 
palpable masses. Extraocular movements are full, facial expressions are symmetrical, respirations are not 
labored. 
Neurological: She is alert and oriented to person, place, and time. 
Skin: Skin is warm and dry. She is not diaphoretic. 
Psychiatric: She has a normal mood and affect. Her behavior is normal.

Clinical Notes (continued)
I have spent 25 minutes in consultation with the patient today. I have also spent time extensively reviewing imaging 
with the patient. Her x-ray showed the instrumentation in good position, unchanged to her previous set of films. Based 
on her symptoms and radiology, I believe that the patient continues to do well post-operatively. We have discussed the 
soreness from her surgery and I have advised her to avoid any turning involving the cervical spine. If her pain persists, 
I have advised her to see Dr. Shields for further evaluation. We have discussed the do's and don'ts and I have 
answered all of her questions. The patient will follow up in 3 months. 
Return in about 3 months (around [DOB-3421]).

No documentation.
Injection Instructions
If you received an injection, a small reaction is not uncommon. A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for a fever of 101.0°F or higher at any
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

MEDICAL RECORDS
To request a copy of your medical records, email the Use and Disclosure Department through your MyNortonChart
account by using the Ask Customer Service feature, or
complete a Norton Healthcare authorization form at https://medicalrecordrequest.nortonhealthcare.org
Once received, your request will be processed within 7 business days.
Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart.
Norton eCare providers are available 24 hours a day, seven days a week through two secure online options using your
MyNortonChart account: a video visit or an eVisit.
For additional information on conditions treated and how to schedule an eCare appointment, visit
NortonHealthcare.com/eCare.
Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record
for their own personal use, including lab results, allergies, medications, and immunization history.
For instructions, visit: https://open.epic.com/Tutorial/PatientAuthentication.

[DOB-3431] 4:54 PM by Amy Mills
September 10, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and then think of nonurgent questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel free to use the attached questionnaire 
to ask those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent 
questions you may have. 
Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we hope you will 
take the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your feedback to improve our patient 
care services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare emergency 
department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.

Clinical Notes
She is about 3-week status post left SLAP repair. She said that the shoulder feels great.
On exam the incisions are healed. She has full and unencumbered range of motion. She is not wearing her sling 
as recommended
Okay to begin physical therapy. I have cautioned her to continue to be careful even though she feels better she is not 
fully healed
Follow-up as needed

Results Callback
If you had labs or diagnostic tests ordered and you do not receive a phone call, letter or MyChart message regarding
your results within 2 weeks, please contact our office to receive your results.
Work Return/Restrictions
No documentation.
Injection Instructions
if you received an injection, asmall reaction is not uncommon.A low grade fever (up to 100.5°F) and some pain is also
not uncommon during the first 24 hours, but unusual beyond. Call the office for afever of101.0°F or higher atany
time. Any expanding redness or persistent drainage from your injection site should be brought to the attention of your
doctor
After-Hours Instructions
For after-hours care, please call the office number and you will be routed to an on-call provider.

Norton eCare
Skip the waiting room for your non urgent health issue using Norton eCare through MyNortonChart

Third Party Apps and Epic
Patients of providers that use Epic software are able to configure 3rd party apps to retrieve parts of their health record

Messages (continued)
September 10, 2020 
Dear Ms. Mills:
Thank you for visiting our office today. We understand sometimes you leave the office and then think of non-urgent 
questions you wish you had asked. Well, we’re here for you. Feel free to use the attached questionnaire to ask 
those questions via MyNortonChart. A staff member or provider will be happy to answer any non-urgent questions 
you may have. Your feedback is very important to us! You may be receiving a survey through e-mail and we hope you will take 
the time to complete it. We value what you have to say and use your feedback to improve our patient care 
services here at Norton Healthcare. Thank you!
If this is a medical emergency, please call 911 or go to the nearest Norton Healthcare emergency 
department. 
We are privileged to be your choice for medical care. Wishing you good health.
From
Cyna Khalily, MD
To
Amy Mills

MILLS, AMY DOB: Dec 28, 1983 (39 yo F) Acc No. 24713959
1. Follow up; headaches
General:
Ms. Amy Mills is a36 y/o F who presents to visit for follow up. She has a
history of chronic migraine.
More recently, she had left shoulder surgery last month. She was also
noted on EMG in 12/2019 to have a chronic left cervical radiculopathy. She
is most bothered by left posterior headaches, lasting hours, associated with
nausea, blurred vision, and photophobia. She has had extensive workup for
this in the past including brain MRI.
She takes Phenergan and Zofran prn for nausea. She takes Amitriptyline
10 mg ghs for headache prevention. She takes Maxalt prn for breakthrough
headaches.
She hasalot ofdifficulty with fatigue and headaches.
WT 172.6, BP 126/84 mmHg, Temp 96.6 F, HR 109 /min, O2 Sat % 98.
General Examination:
GENERAL APPEARANCE: Alert and oriented, no acute distress.
HEAD: Normocephalic, atraumatic.
EYES: Conjunctiva clear and lids normal, EOMI.
NECK/THYROID: Supple.
EXTREMITIES: without clubbing or cyanosis. No periph edema.
MUSCULOSKELETAL: no tenderness with palpation.
SKIN: no rashes, lesions, or ulcerations.
Neuro Exam:
HIGHER FUNCTIONS alert, oriented x3, fluent, no dysarthria.
CRANIAL NERVES PERRL, EOM's intact, VFF, face symmetric, CN II -
XTI intact.
CEREBELLAR normal gait and station.
Romberg sign absent.
FTN intact b/1.
PERIPHERAL EXAM motor exam: strength: 5/5 bilateral upper and
lower
extremities with normal tone
sensation intact to PP, LT, and proprioception bilateral upper and lower
extremities.

G43.719 (Primary)

# Detail Type Description
 1. Assessment Cervicalgia (M54.2).
Impression Medications reviewed. Take medications as prescribed. Side effects discussed and if
experience any side effect stop medication and return to clinic. Counsel on medication
compliance. Advised of proper body mechanics. No heavy lifting over 10 lbs. No
repetitive bending at waist. Change positions frequently, may apply heat/ice to affected
area for 20 minutes for every 3-4 hours. Discussed benefits of maintaining healthy
weight and , if no improvement return one week, will discuss ordering physical therapy if
needed at next visit. If develop any signs/symptoms of cauda equina syndrome such as
loss of bowel loss of bladder ; urinary retention; severe numbness/tingling in both legs
call 911/go to Emergency room immediately. Patient verbalized understanding of plan..
Patient Plan Treatment plan discussed with patient. All questions addressed and answered. 
Instructed to follow up as needed if condition persist or worsens. Patient/caregiver
verbalized understanding.

No taper at this time. Patient to f/u with PCP next month.

Mills, Amy 000000023359 [DOB-5810] [DOB-3657] 11:30 AM 1/7
 2. Assessment Other headache syndrome (G44.89).
Impression Headaches fairly controlled 
No changes to current medications
Monitor and adjust as needed..
Patient Plan How to alleviate headaches
-Apply ice. Hold a cold cloth or an ice pack to the painful area on your head or neck.
Wrap the ice pack in a towel to protect your skin.
-Relaxation exercises. Try relaxation exercises to lower stress.
-Biofeedback. Biofeedback may improve your headaches by helping you to monitor how
your body responds to stress.
-Acupuncture. Acupuncture may improve your headaches and help you relax.
-Take over-the-counter pain relievers. Your doctor may recommend you take
nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), such as naproxen (Aleve) or ibuprofen
(Advil, Motrin IB, others). These medications may relieve your pain soon after your
headache begins.
-Take triptans. Your doctor may prescribe triptans, medications that block pain signals in
your brain. Triptans often may relieve pain from your headache within two hours and
help to control vomiting.
 3. Assessment Body mass index (BMI) 30.0-30.9, adult (Z68.30).

3. Neck pain 
The severity of the problem is moderate. The problem has worsened. The frequency of pain
is daily. Location of pain is bilateral posterior neck. The patient describes the pain as Aching
and Throbbing. The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom do not include
injury. Aggravating factors include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include
joint pain, muscle spasm and tenderness.
4. Left Arm Pain 
It occurs constantly and is fluctuating. Location: left. The pain is aching and throbbing. 
Context: there is an injury. The pain is aggravated by movement, walking and standing. The
pain is relieved by pain/RX meds. Associated symptoms include decreased mobility and joint
instability.

Assessment/Plan
# Detail Type Description
 1. Assessment Cervicalgia (M54.2), chronic, Recurrent.
Impression Medications reviewed. Take medications as prescribed. Side effects discussed and if
experience any side effect stop medication and return to clinic. Counsel on medication
compliance. Advised of proper body mechanics. No heavy lifting over 10 lbs. No
repetitive bending at waist. Change positions frequently, may apply heat/ice to affected
area for 20 minutes for every 3-4 hours. Discussed benefits of maintaining healthy
weight and , if no improvement return one week, will discuss ordering physical therapy if
needed at next visit. If develop any signs/symptoms of cauda equina syndrome such as
loss of bowel loss of bladder ; urinary retention; severe numbness/tingling in both legs
call 911/go to Emergency room immediately. Patient verbalized understanding of plan..
Patient Plan Treatment plan discussed with patient. All questions addressed and answered. 
Instructed to follow up as needed if condition persist or worsens. Patient/caregiver
verbalized understanding.
Provider Plan Hydrocodone 7.5/325 (#60) take one tablet every 12 hours as needed
Followed by Neurosurgeon in Louisville
No taper at this time. Patient to f/u with PCP next month.
Kasper, UDS, and controlled narcotic agreement obtained/on chart.
Plan Orders follow-up visit 2 Months.
 2. Assessment Chronic left shoulder pain (M25.512), chronic, Recurrent.
Patient Plan Treatment plan discussed with patient. All questions addressed and answered. 
Instructed to follow up as needed if condition persist or worsens. Patient/caregiver
verbalized understanding.

not to exceed 3 doses in 24
hours

Impression Medications reviewed, Take medications aas prescribed. Side effects discussed and if
experience any side effect stop medication and return to clinic. Counsel on medication
compliance. Advised of proper body mechanics. No heavy lifting over 10 Ibs. No
repetitive bending at waist. Change positions frequently, may apply heat/ice to affected
area for 20 minutes for every 3-4 hours. Discussed benefits of maintaining healthy
weight and, if no improvement return one week, will discuss ordering physical therapy if
needed at next visit. If develop any signs/symptoms of cauda equina syndrome such as
loss of bowel loss of bladder; urinary retention; severe numbness/tingling in both legs
call |911/go. to Emergency room immediately. Patient verbalized understanding ¢of plan..
plan discussed with patient. All questions. addressed and answered.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91106376531 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. | further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital will be
provided to my primary care physician on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, | understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom it does not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.
4. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that | am

emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a 
stroke.

Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you

Date: [DOB-4011] 09:30 AM
Visit Type: Office Visit
This 37 year old female presents for MA Notes.
History of Present Illness
1. MA Notes 
Pt is here for follow up appointment and medication refills.
2. Chronic Conditions 
*See Chronic Conditions HPI
3. Neck pain 
The severity of the problem is moderate. The problem has worsened. The frequency of pain
is daily. Location of pain is bilateral posterior neck. The patient describes the pain as Aching
and Throbbing. The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom do not include
injury. Aggravating factors include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include
joint pain, muscle spasm and tenderness. Pertinent negatives include weight loss.
4. Left shoulder pain 
It occurs constantly and is fluctuating. Location: left shoulder. The pain is aching and
throbbing. Context: there is an injury. The pain is aggravated by movement. The pain is
relieved by pain/RX meds. Associated symptoms include decreased mobility, joint instability,
joint tenderness and nocturnal awakening. Hand Dominance: left.
Chronic Conditions Addressed Today
Diagnosis Description Code Status HPI Comments
Cervicalgia M54.2 Stable

Mills, Amy 000000023359 [DOB-5810] [DOB-4151] 04:05 PM 1/8
Assessment/Plan
# Detail Type Description
 1. Assessment Acute laryngopharyngitis (J06.0).
Patient Plan There's no cure for the common cold. Antibiotics are of no use against cold viruses and
shouldn't be used unless there's a bacterial infection.
To make yourself as comfortable as possible when you have a cold, try:
Drinking plenty of fluids-Water, juice, clear broth or warm lemon water are good
choices. Avoid caffeine and alcohol, which can dehydrate you.
Eating chicken soup-Chicken soup/warm fluids can be soothing and can loosen
congestion.
Resting-If possible, stay home from work or school if you have a fever or a bad cough or
are drowsy after taking medications.
Adjusting your room's temperature and humidity-Keep your room warm, but not
overheated. If the air is dry, a cool-mist humidifier or vaporizer can moisten the air and
help ease congestion and coughing. Keep the humidifier clean to prevent the growth of
bacterial and molds.
Sooth throat-Saltwater gargle can temporarily relieve sore throat.
Saline Nasal Drops-can help relieve nasal congestion, even in children.
Provider Plan Zpack (#1) use as directed
 Delsym (120ml) take 5-10ml every 12 hours as needed for cough
Medrol Dose Pack (#1) use as directed
Plan Orders follow-up visit if not improved. Reason for referral: if not improved.
 2. Assessment Cervicalgia (M54.2), chronic, Recurrent.
Patient Plan Treatment plan discussed with patient. All questions addressed and answered. 
Instructed to follow up as needed if condition persist or worsens. Patient/caregiver
verbalized understanding.

Followed by Neurosurgeon in Louisville
No taper at this time. Patient to f/u with PCP next month.
Kasper, UDS, and controlled narcotic agreement obtained/on chart.
Plan Orders follow-up visit 2 Months.

Visit Type: Office Visit
This 37 year old female presents for MA Notes, Chronic Conditions, Neck Pain and Left Shoulder Pain.
History of Present Illness
1. MA Notes 
Pt is here for follow up and medication refills. Pt c/o pain in L shoulder and neck
2. Chronic Conditions 
*See Chronic Conditions HPI
3. Neck Pain 
The severity of the problem is moderate. The problem has worsened. The frequency of pain
is daily. Location of pain is bilateral posterior neck. The patient describes the pain as Aching
and Throbbing. The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom include injury. 
Aggravating factors include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include decreased
mobility, joint pain, muscle spasm and tenderness.
4. Left Shoulder Pain 
It occurs constantly and is fluctuating. Location: left shoulder. The pain is aching and
throbbing. Context: there is an injury. The pain is aggravated by movement, walking and
standing. The pain is relieved by pain/RX meds. Associated symptoms include decreased
mobility and joint instability. Hand Dominance: left.

1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. | further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital, a
notice of discharge from the hospital, and/or notice of transfer from the hospital to another facility will be provided to my primary care
physician, physician group and/or post acute care provider on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, | understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom it does not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.

16. PROMPT DISCHARGE (INPATIENTS ONLY): | agree to a prompt discharge following an order that medical treatment is completed and
that continued hospitalization is unnecessary. Late discharges may be subject to additional room charges, which may not be covered
by insurance.

undersigned authorizes Baptist to appeal on patient’s behalf any adverse coverage determinations for treatment or services rendered at
Baptist and further authorizes Baptist or its designee to represent patient during any appeal process. The undersigned certifies that he/she
has read and agrees to this form and has received a copy.

Patient Instructions (continued)
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding · Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get 
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a 
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you 
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

2. Neck pain

and Throbbing. The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom do not include
injury. Aggravating factors include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include
joint pain, muscle spasm and tenderness.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91129398428 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. | further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital, a
notice of discharge from the hospital, and/or notice of transfer from the hospital to another facility will be provided to my primary care
physician, physician group and/or post acute care provider on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, | understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom it does not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.

Patient Instructions (continued)
None · Call 911 or have someone take you to the Emergency Department if you have any of the following: · Sudden numbness or weakness of your face, arm or leg especially on one side of the body
· Sudden confusion, difficulty speaking or trouble understanding · Changes in your vision or loss of sight in one eye
· Sudden severe headache with no known cause
· Sudden dizziness, trouble walking, loss of balance or coordination
It is important to seek emergency care right away if you have further stroke symptoms. If you get 
emergency help quickly, the powerful clot-dissolving medicines can reduce the disabilities caused by a 
stroke. 
For more information:
American Stroke Association
1-888-4-STROKE
www.strokeassociation.org
IF YOU SMOKE OR USE TOBACCO PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING:
Why is smoking bad for me?
Smoking increases the risk of heart disease, lung disease, vascular disease, stroke, and cancer. If you 
smoke, STOP!
For more information:
Quit Now Kentucky
1-800-QUIT-NOW
https://kentucky.quitlogix.org/en-US/
If you feel like life is too tough and are thinking of suicide or injuring yourself, get help right away! · Call or text 988 to speak to someone.

This 38 year old patient presents for GYN US.
History of Present Illness
1. GYN US 
Ultrasound today shows nothing unusual although we are not able to see her ovaries well due
to an increase of overlying bowel. Her H. pylori test is negative. Her symptoms seem more GI
in nature than gynecologic and she is status post hysterectomy. She is not having any
prolapse issues. I think she would benefit from a GI referral.

Visit Type: Office Visit
This 38 year old patient presents for MA Notes, Neck pain and Left Shoulder pain.
History of Present Illness
1. MA Notes 
Pt is being seen because her chart needs to be up to date for her neuro surgeon and to
schedule an MRI.
2. Neck pain 
The severity of the problem is moderate. The problem has worsened. The frequency of pain
is daily. Location of pain is bilateral posterior neck. The patient describes the pain as Aching
and Throbbing. The event(s) surrounding the occurrence of the symptom do not include
injury. Aggravating factors include flexion and turning head. Associated symptoms include
joint pain, muscle spasm and tenderness.
3. Left Shoulder pain 
It occurs occasionally and is fluctuating. Location: left shoulder. The pain is aching and
throbbing. Context: there is no injury. The pain is aggravated by movement. Associated
symptoms include decreased mobility. Hand Dominance: left.

MRI Humerus Left Without Contrast [470911402] Resulted: 05/27/22 0806, Result status: Final result
Ordering provider: Gross, Theresa, APRN 05/26/22 1323 Order status: Completed
Resulted by: Daniel, William T II, MD Filed by: Interface, Rad Results Enterprise In 05/27/22 0809
Performed: 05/26/22 1328 - 05/26/22 1427 Accession number: [PHONE-4847]
Resulting lab: BAPTIST HEALTH RADIOLOGY
Narrative:
MRI HUMERUS LEFT WO CONTRASTREASON FOR EXAM: M25.512; M54.2-Cervicalgia; M25.512-Pain in left
shoulder
COMPARISON: Plain films of the humerus dated [DOB-4848]
Scans were obtained through the left humerus in the sagittal, axial and
coronal planes using T1 and T2-weighted scans.
MRI Findings: In the mid shaft of the humerus there is some increased
signal focally in the cortical bone of the humerus. This increased
signal is on both the T1 and T2 scans. The marrow signal was
unremarkable. There is no edema in the area of the periosteum. Muscles
in the arm were unremarkable. No other areas of abnormal signal are
noted in the humerus.
Impression:
Focal abnormal signal in the cortical bone of the mid shaft
of humerus on both the T1 and T2 sequences. This could represent
hemangioma. Would recommend consideration of bone scan especially in the
setting of pain in this region for further evaluation.
This report was finalized on 5/27/2022 8:06 AM by Dr. William Daniel II,
MD.

INPATIENT AND OUTPATIENT CONDITIONS AND CONSENTS CSN: 91131427492 ADMIT DATE:
1. CONSENT TO DIAGNOSTIC TESTS, PROCEDURES, & MEDICALTREATMENT:| voluntarily consent to (and consent on behalf of any
child born to me while hospitalized) care involving routine diagnostic tests, procedures and medical treatment as ordered by my
admitting and treating physicians, including their assistants or designees. | consent to receive medical care through the use of
telehealth and/or remote patient monitoring. | further consent to the interpretation of diagnostic studies from an off-site location using
telehealth technologies. | consent to medical, nursing, allied health and other students observing and participating in my care under the
supervision of a qualified professional. | consent to photographic recordings or reproducible images during the surgical, medical, and/or
diagnostic procedure(s) and their use for identification, scientific, educational, or research purposes. | also consent to testing for
communicable and blood-borne infectious diseases, such as hepatitis, tuberculosis, and the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), if a
provider orders testing for diagnostic purposes or if there has been an exposure to healthcare personnel. | have been given no
guarantees about the results that may be obtained from my care. | understand and agree that the results of diagnostic tests, procedures
and medical treatment ordered and provided by providers while | am hospitalized, including a notice of admission to the hospital, a
notice of discharge from the hospital, and/or notice of transfer from the hospital to another facility will be provided to my primary care
physician, physician group and/or post acute care provider on record, if any.
2. PHYSICIANS AND OTHER HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT EMPLOYEES OF THE HOSPITAL: Physicians and certified
registered nurse anesthetists rendering medical treatment to patients in the hospital are not employed by the Hospital. In most
situations, advanced practitioners, including but not limited to, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, surgical assistants and
psychologists, providing treatment to patients in the Hospital are not employed by the Hospital. You may receive a separate bill for the
services of these providers, or the Hospital bill you receive may have separate charges for services of such providers. Charges for such
services are established by the providers.
3. FINANCIAL AGREEMENT AND ASSIGNMENT OF BENEFITS:| agree that | am responsible for payment of my Hospital bill. Payment
of any portion of my bill not covered by a third party payor is due upon discharge from the Hospital unless the Hospital has agreed to
other arrangements. | agree to the assignment of all third party payor benefits to the Hospital and to any health care provider rendering
services to me. | acknowledge and agree that Hospital is not required to accept assignment of any third party payor benefits, in which
case, | may receive a bill from Hospital for the full amount of charges related to any care or treatment provided to me or my guarantee by
Hospital and | agree to pay the Hospital and other health care providers for such charges. Moreover, | understand that Hospital may
accept payment from third party payors with whom it does not have a written signed agreement and that such acceptance of payment
does not constitute acceptance by Hospital of any reimbursement rates established by such third party payors and that | may receive a
bill from Hospital for the difference between the rate paid by such third party payors and the Hospital’s charges. | agree to pay the
Hospital and other health care providers for all charges for services that are not covered or paid by any third party payor regardless of the
reason, including but not limited to a determination by any third party payor that such services are not covered services or medically
necessary. To the extent | am a Medicare or Medicaid beneficiary, | certify that any information | provide in applying for payment under
Title XVIII (Medicare) or Title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act is correct. | request payment of authorized benefits to be made on
my behalf to Baptist Health facility or to any health care provider rendering services to me by the Medicare or Medicaid program. |
hereby irrevocably appoint Hospital as my authorized representative to pursue any claims, penalties, and administrative and/or legal
remedies on my behalf for collection against any responsible payer, employer-sponsored medical benefit plans, third party liability
carrier or, any other responsible third party for any and all benefits due me for the payment of charges associated with my treatment.
This assignment shall not be construed as an obligation of the Hospital to pursue any such right of recovery. | agree to take all actions
necessary to assist Hospital in collecting payment from any such third party payer. | understand that after reasonable notice, any
unpaid account may be turned over to a collection agency and/or attorney for collection. Should it be necessary for the Hospital to
pursue collection, | agree to pay all reasonable collection costs, including court costs and attorney’s fees incurred by the Hospital in
collecting my account. Pursuant to the Fair Credit Reporting Act (15 USC §1681b(a)(2)), | authorize a credit reporting agency engaged
by Hospital or its collection agents to release to the Hospital or any of its representatives or affiliates, my consumer report. |
understand that the purpose of this authorization and request is to obtain my consumer report, which may be used to determine the
availability of or the need for financial assistance, charity care, or insurance coverage for me and may be used for billing and collection
purposes related to payment for services provided to me. | understand this authorization is valid until my Hospital account for all
services is closed.

. OUTPATIENT MEDICARE PATIENTS: Medicare does not cover prescription drugs with only a few exceptions. | acknowledge that | am
responsible for any drugs furnished to me while an outpatient that meet Medicare’s definition of a prescription drug. These drugs are
also referred to as self-administered but they may be administered to me by Hospital personnel. Hospital may bill Medicare patients or
other third party payers for these drugs. Medicare Part D beneficiaries may submit a paper claim to the Medicare Part D Plan for
possible reimbursement of these drugs in accordance with Medicare Drug Plan enrollment materials.

PROPERTY.
. SMOKE-FREE ENVIRONMENT: This Hospital maintains a smoke-free environment on its campus. Smoking is prohibited by health care

treatment area may complicate or interfere with the course of treatment prescribed by my physician.

Patient Plan May use OTC analgesics such as Tylenol or Ibuprofen, per package directions, for pain.

[DOB-5011] - NM cor bone scan limited in BAPTIST HEALTH CORBIN NUCLEAR MEDICINE (continued)
Imaging (continued)
CLINICAL INDICATION: CHRONIC PAIN IN LEFT SHOULDER; M25.511-Pain in
right shoulder; G89.29-Other chronic pain
TECHNIQUE: 17.2 mCi of Tc-99m HDP were injected IV. Several hours later,
small field-of-view imaging centered on the chest and shoulders was
performed.
COMPARISON: NONE 
FINDINGS: Low level increased tracer uptake of both shoulders in a
relatively symmetric pattern most consistent with arthritic type change.
Low level increased tracer uptake of the sternoclavicular joint regions
and of the thoracic spine also most consistent with degenerative type
uptake. No intense areas of tracer activity noted in the humerus shaft
corresponding to abnormality on MRI. Both kidneys are visualized. 
Impression:
1. No focal areas of increased tracer uptake involving the mid left
humeral diaphysis corresponding to abnormality seen on previous MRI.
2. Degenerative type uptake noted of the axial and appendicular
skeleton.
This report was finalized on 7/5/2022 2:37 PM by Dr. Kevin J Croce, MD.

COMPARISON: Plain films of the humerus dated [DOB-5129]
Scans were obtained through the left humerus in the sagittal, axial and
coronal planes using T1 and T2-weighted scans.
MRI Findings: In the mid shaft of the humerus there is some increased
signal focally in the cortical bone of the hurnerus. This increased
signal is on both the T1 and T2 scans. The marrow signal was
unremarkable. There is no edema in the area of the periosteum. Muscles
in the arm were unremarkable. No other areas of abnormal signal are
noted in the humerus.
IMPRESSION:
Focal abnormal signal in the cortical bone of the mid shaft
of humerus on both the Ti and T2 sequences. This could represent
hemangioma. Would recommend consideration of bone scan especially in the
setting of pain in this region for further evaluation.
